age.short_description: Age group for measles case stratification.
age.long_description: Age group categories used for measles case reporting: 0-4 years, 5-19 years, 20+ years, and Unknown age.

cum_cases_measles_age.short_name: Cumulative measles cases by age group
cum_cases_measles_age.long_name: Cumulative measles cases in the United States by age group
cum_cases_measles_age.short_description: Cumulative count of confirmed measles cases in the United States since the start of the calendar year, stratified by age group.
cum_cases_measles_age.long_description: This dataset contains cumulative counts of confirmed measles cases in the United States, reported by the CDC. Data is stratified by age group (0-4 years, 5-19 years, 20+ years, and Unknown age). Values represent cumulative cases from the start of each calendar year. For 2025, the first data was available from Feb 7, 2025. Data is manually recorded from the CDC measles surveillance page.
cum_cases_measles_age.statement: As of {time}, there have been {value} cumulative measles cases in the {age} age group in the United States.

cdc_measles_age.name: CDC Measles Cases and Outbreaks - Age Distribution
cdc_measles_age.organization: Centers for Disease Control and Prevention
cdc_measles_age.description: Cumulative measles case counts stratified by age group, reported on the CDC Measles Cases and Outbreaks surveillance page. Age groups include 0-4 years, 5-19 years, 20+ years, and Unknown. Data is updated weekly as part of CDC's enhanced measles surveillance program. This age-stratified data helps public health officials understand which populations are most affected by measles outbreaks and identify gaps in vaccination coverage. Cases are classified as confirmed or probable following Council of State and Territorial Epidemiologists (CSTE) case definitions.
cdc_measles_age.restrictions: Public domain. CDC data is generally not subject to copyright restrictions.
